cdc_advisory: HAN-00530 (Health Alert); enhanced travel screening from 2026-05-18
who_status: PHEIC declared 2026-05-17 (Public Health Emergency of International Concern)
notes: Bundibugyo virus identified by INRB; cases span 13 health zones across Ituri / Nord-Kivu / Sud-Kivu provinces (Ituri remains epicenter; Sud-Kivu first lab-confirmed case 2026-05-25). CDC Travel Notice escalated from Level 2 to Level 3 after the 2026-05-16 CDC Situation Summary publication. Africa CDC declared a Public Health Emergency of Continental Security (PHECS) on 2026-05-18 (https://africacdc.org/news-item/u-s-travel-restrictions-related-to-the-bundibugyo-ebola-outbreak/). US State Department Travel Advisory at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) for DRC; US entry restrictions in effect from 2026-05-22 on non-US passport holders who travelled to DRC in the previous 21 days, extended to green-card holders the same week; CDC Title 42 Order in effect for arrivals from DRC (https://www.cdc.gov/media/releases/2026/statement-update-on-title-42-order.html). US arrivals from affected countries funneled through IAD (from 2026-05-21), ATL (from 2026-05-22), and IAH Houston (from 2026-05-26) for enhanced screening. On 2026-05-31 the DRC government and WHO issued a joint statement explicitly asking other governments to keep borders open (https://www.who.int/news/item/31-05-2026-joint-statement-by-the-government-of-the-democratic-republic-of-the-congo-and-who-concerning-the-outbreak-of-ebola-disease-caused-by-the-bundibugyo-virus). 2026-06-15: CDC re-reviewed the DRC Level 3 notice (page now dated 15 June 2026); the affected provinces (Ituri, North Kivu, South Kivu) remain Level 3 (Reconsider Nonessential Travel), with non-affected DRC provinces now covered under the combined DRC+Uganda Level 2 notice (https://wwwnc.cdc.gov/travel/notices/level2/ebola-drc-uganda). DRC Level 3 URL and level unchanged. 2026-07-12: the DRC Institut National de Sante Publique formally designated Haut-Uele and Tshopo as epidemic zones (4 cases including 2 deaths in Tshopo, 1 death in Haut-Uele, all judged imported from Nia Nia HZ in Ituri), taking the outbreak from 3 to 5 affected provinces (Reuters via CNBC Africa https://www.cnbcafrica.com/2026/congo-says-ebola-outbreak-spreads-to-provinces-haut-uele-and-tshopo). CDC has NOT re-scoped its Level 3 notice, which still names only Ituri, Nord-Kivu and Sud-Kivu; watch for a re-scope. No change to cdc_travel_notice_level, cdc_advisory, or who_status.

case_status: Active outbreak (imported plus local transmission); four-week border closure with DRC declared 2026-05-27
cdc_advisory: HAN-00530 (Health Alert); enhanced travel screening from 2026-05-18
who_status: Included in joint PHEIC declaration of 2026-05-17
notes: Initial cases imported from DRC (May 15-16); healthcare-worker cases in Kampala were contacts under follow-up per Uganda MoH (not undetected community transmission). 2026-05-27: Uganda ordered border with DRC closed "with immediate effect" for four weeks (Ebola response teams, humanitarian, cargo, security exempted, subject to mandatory 21-day isolation). Measure goes against WHO guidance, which warns closures push movement to unmonitored informal crossings. 2026-05-27: CDC raised Uganda Travel Health Notice from Level 1 (Practice Usual Precautions) to Level 2 (Practice Enhanced Precautions), per the US Embassy Kampala 28 May health alert (https://ug.usembassy.gov/health-alert-u-s-embassy-kampala-uganda-may-28-2026/). US State Department Travel Advisory for Uganda at Level 4 (Do Not Travel). US entry restrictions on non-US passport holders who travelled to Uganda in the previous 21 days (from 2026-05-22, extended to green-card holders same week); CDC Title 42 Order in effect for arrivals from Uganda. Africa CDC declared a Public Health Emergency of Continental Security (PHECS) on 2026-05-18 covering both DRC and Uganda. 2026-06-15: CDC re-reviewed the Bundibugyo Travel Health Notices (both per-country pages now dated 15 June 2026) and consolidated the standalone Uganda Level 2 notice into a combined DRC+Uganda Level 2 notice at https://wwwnc.cdc.gov/travel/notices/level2/ebola-drc-uganda (the old /level2/ebola-uganda URL now redirects there); Uganda level unchanged at Level 2 (Practice Enhanced Precautions). cdc_travel_notice_url updated accordingly.

case_status: Imported case (single; doctor returned from DRC humanitarian mission); isolated, stable. No local transmission.
cdc_advisory: None (HAN-00530 covers DRC/Uganda; no France-specific HAN)
cdc_travel_notice_level: None (no France-specific CDC Travel Health Notice; verified absent from CDC Travel Notices index 2026-06-25)
who_status: Covered under joint PHEIC of 2026-05-17; no France-specific WHO declaration
notes: France reported its first-ever Ebola case on 2026-06-24: an imported case in a doctor who returned from a humanitarian mission in DRC, admitted to a specialised facility in stable condition (French Ministry of Health, carried by Sky News https://news.sky.com/story/france-reports-first-ebola-case-after-doctor-returning-from-dr-congo-tests-positive-13556940 and ABC News). ECDC assessed the risk to the wider European population as low (https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/ebola-virus-disease-outbreak-democratic-republic-congo-and-uganda). Added as a tracked country because the authoritative BNO/WHO Daily Ebola Update graphic folded France into the global outbreak total from the 24 June ("40 days") edition (DR Congo + Uganda + France = TOTAL 1,139 confirmed). FLAG FOR WEB: scope change per METHODOLOGY "Adding a new country"; France carries a single imported case and no France-specific CDC HAN, CDC Travel Health Notice, or WHO declaration yet. If those remain absent and the case does not seed local transmission, Web may prefer to keep France as a country_breakdown/declarations entry without a timeseries breakout column.
